commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: Docker: add Mac migration and keep-awake helpers
target.body:
## What
Add operator helpers for Mac-hosted Docker deployments.

## Why
Running OpenClaw in Docker on macOS is easier to operate if the repo includes a simple host-awake helper and a low-disruption backup/restore path for moving from an Intel Mac to Apple Silicon.

## Included
- add `scripts/openclaw-keepawake.sh` to keep the host awake during long-running gateway sessions
- add `scripts/migrate/backup-openclaw.sh` to capture Docker/OpenClaw state for migration
- add `scripts/migrate/restore-openclaw.sh` to restore that state on the target host
- document the keep-awake and migration workflow in the Docker install guide

## Notes
This PR is intentionally separate from the Docker runtime/image PR so the runtime behavior and the host-operator helpers can be reviewed independently.


## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Mac-hosted Docker operator helpers can preserve OpenClaw config/workspace/scripts for migration and keep the host awake during long-running gateway sessions without touching the runtime-image PR.
- **Real environment tested**: Intel macOS host with Docker Desktop and the existing OpenClaw Docker deployment. The backup helper was used to create `backups/pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz`, and the resulting archive was used as the reference while redeploying the live Docker setup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `bash -n scripts/migrate/backup-openclaw.sh scripts/migrate/restore-openclaw.sh scripts/migrate/lib.sh scripts/openclaw-keepawake.sh`, inspected the real backup archive contents with `tar -tzf`, restored Docker `.env` values from the archive after setup rewrites, and verified the redeployed gateway still saw the preserved workspace scripts.
- **Evidence after fix**: redacted terminal output from the real Mac Docker setup:

```text
bash -n scripts/migrate/backup-openclaw.sh scripts/migrate/restore-openclaw.sh scripts/migrate/lib.sh scripts/openclaw-keepawake.sh
passed

ls -l backups/pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz backups/pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz.sha256
pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz        90126085 bytes
pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz.sha256 100 bytes

tar -tzf backups/pre-upstream-sync-20260509.tar.gz | grep selected preserved files
./payload/repo/.env
./payload/repo/docker-compose.yml
./payload/repo/docker-compose.extra.yml
./payload/config/openclaw.json
./payload/config/exec-approvals.json
./payload/workspace/scripts/run_ai_paper_digest.sh
./payload/workspace/scripts/run_portfolio_monitor.sh
./payload/workspace/scripts/paper_digest_scan.py
./payload/workspace/scripts/send_paper_digest.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/send_portfolio_status.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/portfolio_monitor.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/portfolio_sell_monitor.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/portfolio_taxloss_monitor.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/render_portfolio_status.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/tlh_monitor.js
./payload/workspace/scripts/automation_escalation_state.js

restored .env Docker-related values from backup
OPENCLAW_EXTRA_MOUNTS=<len 54>
OPENCLAW_HOME_VOLUME=<len 13>
OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES=<len 267>
OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER=<len 1>
OPENCLAW_DOCKER_EXEC_SECURITY=<len 4>
OPENCLAW_DOCKER_EXEC_ASK=<len 3>
OPENCLAW_DOCKER_EXEC_ASK_FALLBACK=<len 4>
OPENCLAW_SKIP_ONBOARDING=<len 0>

post-redeploy workspace script syntax
syntax-ok
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The helper-generated archive preserved the repo `.env`, Compose files, OpenClaw config, exec approvals, and the digest/portfolio/scanner scripts that the Docker deployment depends on. The updated checksum path tolerated runtime symlinks, and the live deployment was rebuilt afterward with those preserved values restored.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run a destructive restore over the active live config on the same machine; restore behavior was limited to archive inspection and non-destructive syntax/path validation.
target.author: ruslansv

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
